title: (No AI) WordPress.com now lets AI agents write and publish posts, and more

creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Trump’s AI framework targets state laws, shifts child safety burden to parents
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: Trump’s AI framework pushes federal preemption of state laws, emphasizes innovation, and shifts responsibility for child safety toward parents while laying out lighter-touch rules for tech companies.
contentSnippet: Trump’s AI framework pushes federal preemption of state laws, emphasizes innovation, and shifts responsibility for child safety toward parents while laying out lighter-touch rules for tech companies.

title: (No AI) Google Search is now using AI to replace headlines
author: Sean Hollister
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/10455389/mdoying_180117_2249_0206stills.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Since roughly the turn of the millennium, Google Search has been the bedrock of the web. People loved Google's trustworthy "10 blue links" search experience and its unspoken promise: The website you click is the website you get.

Now, Google is beginning to replace news headlines in its search results with ones that are AI-generated. After doing something similar [in its Google Discover news feed](https://www.theverge.com/tech/865168/google-says-ai-news-headlines-are-feature-not-experiment), it's starting to mess with headlines in the traditional "10 blue links," too. We've found multiple examples where Google replaced headlines we wrote with ones we did not, sometimes changing their meaning in the process.

For example, Google reduced …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/896490/google-replace-news-headlines-in-search-canary-coal-mine-experiment)

Now, Google is beginning to replace news headlines in its search results with ones that are AI-generated. After doing something similar in its Google Discover news feed, it's starting to mess with headlines in the traditional "10 blue links," too. We've found multiple examples where Google replaced headlines we wrote with ones we did not, sometimes changing their meaning in the process.
For example, Google reduced  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Amazon is making an Alexa phone
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![An illustration of the Amazon logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/09/acastro_STK103__03.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Over 10 years after shelving the Fire Phone, Amazon is reportedly planning to launch another smartphone, this time focused on Alexa. According to [_Reuters_](https://www.reuters.com/technology/amazon-plans-smartphone-comeback-more-than-decade-after-fire-phone-flop-2026-03-20/), the phone, which is code-named "Transformer," will center around Amazon's AI assistant, but Alexa won't "necessarily be the primary operating system of the phone."

"Transformer" is currently in development in Amazon's ZeroOne group, led by J Allard, who previously worked on the Zune and Xbox at Microsoft. Allard's team has reportedly explored both smartphone and "dumbphone" designs, taking inspiration from the $700 [minimalist Light Phone](https://www.theverge.com/reviews/637178/light-phone-iii-review-minimalist-smartphone), which features a black-and-white display and lack …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/897915/amazon-transformer-alexa-phone)

"Transformer" is currently in development in Amazon's ZeroOne group, led by J Allard, who previously worked on the Zune and Xbox at Microsoft. Allard's team has reportedly explored both smartphone and "dumbphone" designs, taking inspiration from the $700 minimalist Light Phone, which features a black-and-white display and lack …

creator: Jaclyn Greenberg
title: (No AI) I Learned More Than I Thought I Would From Using Food-Tracking Apps
dc:creator: Jaclyn Greenberg
content: These apps, some of which use AI and computer vision, were helpful for meeting my caloric and nutrition intake goals. But they also gave me some anxiety.
contentSnippet: These apps, some of which use AI and computer vision, were helpful for meeting my caloric and nutrition intake goals. But they also gave me some anxiety.
